blog_posts.author: Avery Quinn
blog_posts.excerpt: How to structure blog indexes and search results for faster scanning and better comprehension.

blog_posts.title: Rich vs Simple Lists: Picking the Right Variant for Your Use Case
blog_posts.author: Maya Chen
blog_posts.excerpt: Simple lists are efficient for navigation and TOCs; rich lists shine for content discovery like blogs.

blog_posts.author: Jordan Patel
blog_posts.excerpt: A practical approach to pairing infinite scroll with crawlable, paginated URLs and accessible navigation.

blog_posts.title: Compact vs Relaxed Density: Balancing Speed and Comfort
blog_posts.author: Avery Quinn
blog_posts.excerpt: Use compact density for high-intent scans like search; relaxed density for exploratory reading like blogs.

blog_posts.title: Metadata Hierarchy: Date, Author, Category—What Users Actually Notice
blog_posts.author: Kai Morgan
blog_posts.excerpt: Clarify roles of metadata and avoid clutter by styling them as secondary to titles.

search_results.title: List vs Grid: Choosing the Right Layout for Content
search_results.snippet: Lists are optimal for text-heavy items and scanning by title; grids excel for visual browsing.

search_results.title: Documentation: List Component API and Variants
search_results.snippet: API for simple, rich, table-like, and thumbnail list variants with props and accessibility notes.

search_results.title: Search UX: Improving Relevance with Better Snippets
search_results.snippet: Snippets should preview the answer, not repeat the title; keep them to 1–2 succinct lines.

search_results.title: SEO Guide: Paginated URLs for Infinite Scroll
search_results.snippet: Pair infinite scroll with crawlable pagination to preserve indexing and shareable positions.

search_results.title: Accessibility Checklist for List-Based Pages
search_results.snippet: Ensure list semantics, logical focus order, and 44px touch targets for all interactive elements.
